title: Operator-collaboration gates in simulated runs
prompt: How should the runs the simulator drives handle operator-collaboration gates (Spec holds, checkpoints, questions)?
context: Carrying forward your June frame decisions unchanged: full 4-phase scope, record locally → committed fixtures → static replay on the site, new crate `crates/darkrun-sim`, player in `web/site`. One new wrinkle from discovery: in team/solo modes every station's Spec phase **holds** until `darkrun_elaborate_seal`, and checkpoints wait on operator decisions — so a zero-knowledge prompt-follower needs either an operator in the loop or a `dark`-mode run (pre-elaborated, linear, no per-station collab hold). The engine's deadlock guard (`Escalate` after 4 no-progress ticks) is the stranded verdict either way.

options.label: Dark-mode spine first, operator-sim later (Recommended)
options.description: The recorder's v1 fixtures drive dark-mode runs (linear, no collaboration hold) — protocol fidelity of the prompt chain gets tested end-to-end with the least scope. A scripted operator-sim (answers questions, decides checkpoints, triggers seals) arrives as a later phase, unlocking solo-mode gate-surfacing coverage.

options.label: Operator-sim in the spine from day one
options.description: Build the scripted operator-sim into phase 1 so solo-mode runs (gates, checkpoints, questions) are simulated immediately. Fuller coverage of the gate surface earlier, at the cost of a bigger spine before anything is demoable.

options.label: Dark mode only — operator gates permanently out of scope
options.description: The simulator only ever drives dark-mode runs; gate/checkpoint surfacing stays covered by the existing e2e + manual use, and the sim stays maximally small.
